proposal.title: Contract coverage penalty inverted — unresolved dependents reduce risk score
proposal.whyThisBoundary: contractCoverageAdjustment is the risk scoring component that boosts scores when a contract change (signature_changed, export_changed, entity_deleted) has unresolved direct dependents. The blue team's risk tests only validate range [0,100] and descending sort — never that unresolved dependents produce HIGHER scores. Inverting the penalty to a reward goes undetected.
proposal.survivalRationale: With the mutation, add's score drops from 70 to 50 (penalty=10 becomes delta=-10 instead of +10). Blast dependent sum scores 63 unchanged. Final sorted order [63, 55, 50] is still descending, all in [0,100], all have factors. The test at line 430 never checks that directly-changed contract-breaking entities score higher than their dependents. This completely inverts the risk signal: entities with MORE unresolved dependents get LOWER scores.
proposal.validatorNotes: Single token change: positive delta becomes negative. Only affects the unresolved-dependents return path in contractCoverageAdjustment. The other two return paths (non-contract changes return delta:0, no-dependents returns delta:-10) are unchanged.
